_metadata.description: Multi-vendor GPU WebGPU/Vulkan capabilities and limits reference
notes: WebGPU limits are browser-reported tiers, not raw hardware limits (anti-fingerprinting).
Vulkan limits from vulkan.gpuinfo.org driver reports.
WebGPU maxBufferSize/maxStorageBufferBindingSize are spec defaults; request higher via requiredLimits.
shader-f16 requires VK_KHR_shader_float16_int8 + shaderFloat16 on Vulkan, or native Metal FP16 on Apple.
Wave/subgroup sizes reflect Vulkan subgroupSize; VK_EXT_subgroup_size_control may allow alternate sizes.
TFLOPS values are peak theoretical FP32 single-precision.

maxStorageBuffersPerShaderStage.note: Browsers report tiered values for anti-fingerprinting. Sum of maxStorageBuffersPerShaderStage + maxStorageTexturesPerShaderStage + maxColorAttachments <= Vulkan maxFragmentCombinedOutputResources.

maxStorageTexturesPerShaderStage.note: Part of the combined output resources budget.

maxBufferSize.note: Must explicitly request higher limit via requiredLimits at device creation.

maxStorageBufferBindingSize.note: Chrome often caps at 128 MiB unless explicitly requested. Some reports show ~2 GiB achievable on high-VRAM NVIDIA cards.

vulkan_limits_nvidia_common._note: These Vulkan limits are consistent across all NVIDIA desktop GPUs (Pascal through Ada Lovelace) with recent drivers.
vulkan_limits_nvidia_common.subgroupSize_note: NVIDIA warp size is always 32 across all architectures.

ada_lovelace.shader_f16_note: Full VK_KHR_shader_float16_int8 shaderFloat16 support. WebGPU shader-f16 available in Chrome 120+.

webgpu_limits.note: Values achievable when explicitly requested via requiredLimits. Default tier values are lower.
Values achievable when explicitly requested via requiredLimits.
Values achievable when explicitly requested via requiredLimits.
8 GB VRAM constrains practical buffer sizes despite spec limits.

ampere.shader_f16_note: Full VK_KHR_shader_float16_int8 shaderFloat16 support. WebGPU shader-f16 available in Chrome 120+.

webgpu_limits.note: Values achievable when explicitly requested via requiredLimits.
10 GB VRAM model; 12 GB variant (RTX 3080 12GB) also exists.
8 GB VRAM constrains practical buffer sizes.
12 GB VRAM is generous for its tier; good for large scene data.

turing.shader_f16_note: VK_KHR_shader_float16_int8 supported with shaderFloat16=true on recent drivers. WebGPU shader-f16 available in Chrome 120+.

webgpu_limits.note: 8 GB VRAM constrains practical buffer sizes.
8 GB VRAM constrains practical buffer sizes.
6 GB VRAM is the tightest constraint for path tracing workloads.

pascal.hw_ray_tracing_note: No dedicated RT cores. DXR ray tracing can be emulated on shader cores via driver, but performance is extremely poor (3-10x slower than RTX equivalent).
pascal.shader_f16_vulkan_note: VK_KHR_shader_float16_int8 extension supported since driver 417.23 (Dec 2018). shaderFloat16=true available, but no native FP16 ALU throughput advantage -- internally computed as FP32.
pascal.shader_f16_webgpu_note: WebGPU shader-f16 feature may be exposed via Chrome, but offers no performance benefit on Pascal since hardware lacks native FP16 compute units.

GTX_1080.tflops_fp16_note: No native FP16 throughput advantage; FP16 runs at FP32 rate.

webgpu_limits.note: 8 GB VRAM. No hardware RT -- software path tracing only.
8 GB VRAM. No hardware RT.

GTX_1060.vram_variants_note: Also available in 3 GB variant with 1152 CUDA cores.

webgpu_limits.note: 6 GB VRAM is minimum viable for path tracing. 3 GB variant likely too constrained.

nvidia_driver_crashes.description: NVIDIA 572.xx drivers cause WebGPU crashes. Symptoms: 'WebGPU: Software only, hardware acceleration unavailable'.
nvidia_driver_crashes.workaround: Update to latest stable NVIDIA drivers. Check chrome://gpu for hardware acceleration status.

maxStorageBufferBindingSize_128mb_cap.description: Chrome caps maxStorageBufferBindingSize at 128 MiB by default even though hardware supports much more. Causes 'Binding size is larger than the maximum binding size' errors.
maxStorageBufferBindingSize_128mb_cap.workaround: Explicitly request higher limit via requiredLimits when calling requestDevice(). Check adapter.limits first.

canvas_resize_memory_leak.description: Canvas resizing triggers GPU memory leaks (Dawn issue #2520). Memory not released, causing gradual performance degradation.
canvas_resize_memory_leak.workaround: Minimize canvas resize operations. Consider fixed-size render target with CSS scaling.

multi_gpu_limitation.description: Chrome issue #329211593 prevents using multiple GPU adapters simultaneously.
multi_gpu_limitation.workaround: Force discrete GPU via chrome://flags or OS GPU preference settings.

storage_texture_breakage_windows.description: WebGPU context breaks when using storage textures on certain NVIDIA + Windows configurations.
storage_texture_breakage_windows.workaround: Test storage texture support at init; fall back to storage buffers if broken.

linux_vulkan_flag_required.description: WebGPU on Linux requires explicit Vulkan flag to be enabled.
linux_vulkan_flag_required.workaround: Enable chrome://flags/#enable-vulkan. Requires Chrome 121+ on Android, Chrome 113+ on desktop.

timestamp_query_jitter.description: Timestamp query results are intentionally jittered by Chrome for security (timing attack mitigation). Resolution reduced to ~100 microseconds.
timestamp_query_jitter.workaround: Average multiple frames for profiling. Not suitable for sub-microsecond measurements.

maxFragmentCombinedOutputResources_misre.description: Vulkan maxFragmentCombinedOutputResources incorrectly reported by many drivers, affecting how browsers compute WebGPU combined storage/texture/attachment limits.
maxFragmentCombinedOutputResources_misre.workaround: Browser implementations have their own workarounds. Test actual limits at runtime.

pathtracer_recommendations.storage_buffer_strategy: Always request maxStorageBufferBindingSize and maxBufferSize via requiredLimits. Default 128 MiB is insufficient for scene BVH + geometry + textures.

vram_budget_guidance.6gb: Tight. Small scenes only. Compress textures aggressively.
vram_budget_guidance.8gb: Moderate. Medium complexity scenes. Use BC/ASTC compressed textures.
vram_budget_guidance.12gb: Comfortable. Most scenes fit without issue.
vram_budget_guidance.24gb: Abundant. Large open-world scenes with many 4K textures.

pathtracer_recommendations.shader_f16_guidance: Use shader-f16 for intersection math and color accumulation on Turing+ for ~25% ALU improvement and ~50% bandwidth improvement. No benefit on Pascal.
pathtracer_recommendations.rt_core_note: WebGPU does not expose hardware ray tracing (no equivalent to VK_KHR_ray_tracing_pipeline). All path tracing must be done in compute shaders regardless of GPU generation.

rdna3.rt_note: 1.5x ray throughput vs RDNA 2. Hardware BVH traversal + ray/box + ray/triangle intersection.
rdna3.wave_note: RDNA 3 natively prefers Wave32. Wave64 can be better for VOPD dual-issue. VK_EXT_subgroup_size_control exposes both.

vulkan_limits_common.note: AMD RADV and amdvlk both report very high descriptor limits.

webgpu_limits.note: Vulkan backend on Windows/Linux. Very high hardware limits.
Same limits as 7900 XTX.

Radeon_780M.shared_memory: LPDDR5X / DDR5 (up to 8 GB allocated via Smart Access Memory)
Radeon_780M.memory_bandwidth_note: DDR5-5600 dual-channel ~89.6 GB/s; LPDDR5X-7500 ~120 GB/s. Varies by OEM.

webgpu_limits.note: Same RDNA 3 limits but shared memory bandwidth is the bottleneck.

rdna2.rt_note: BVH traversal + ray/box intersection in hardware. Ray/triangle intersection in shader. One RA per CU.
rdna2.wave_note: RDNA 2 native Wave32. Wave64 emulated as 2x Wave32.

webgpu_limits.note: 128 MB Infinity Cache very effective for path tracing working sets that fit.
192-bit bus + 12 GB VRAM can be limiting for high-res path tracing. Infinity Cache (96 MB) mitigates.
Very limited bandwidth for path tracing.

rdna1.rt_note: No hardware ray tracing. Software-only ray tracing.
rdna1.wave_note: First AMD GPU with native Wave32. Wave64 compatibility available.
rdna1.shader_f16_note: RDNA 1 does not support shaderFloat16 in Vulkan. No FP16 acceleration.

webgpu_limits.note: No HW RT, no shader-f16, no Infinity Cache. 8 GB VRAM limits scene complexity.

amd_known_bugs_quirks: Vulkan device lost errors under heavy RT + compute workloads (Godot, BG3, llama.cpp)
Infinity Cache thrashing causes performance cliffs with large working sets exceeding cache size
RADV vs amdvlk vs AMD proprietary driver can show different Vulkan limits and behavior on Linux
Performance dropoff at certain batch sizes in compute workloads (llama.cpp issue #10966)
Wave32 exposed via VK_EXT_subgroup_size_control; default Vulkan subgroup size is 64 (not native 32)
RDNA 1 (5700 XT): no shader-f16, no HW RT, no Infinity Cache -- most limited RDNA generation
APU variants (780M, 680M): shared memory bandwidth severely limits path tracing performance

amd_texture_formats.note: All desktop AMD GPUs support BC only. No ETC2 or ASTC.

amd_optimal_workgroup_size.note: Wave32 native; 64 = 2 wavefronts for good occupancy. 256 works well for compute-heavy shaders.

apple_family_9.simd_note: All Apple GPUs use 32-wide SIMD groups. Fixed, not configurable.
apple_family_9.rt_note: Hardware-accelerated ray/box and ray/triangle intersection. 4x faster than M1/M2 software RT. First in M3 and A17 Pro.
apple_family_9.shader_f16_note: Native FP16 support. 2x FP16 throughput per lane. WebGPU shader-f16 works via Metal backend.

webgpu_limits_common.maxStorageBufferBindingSize_note: Metal backend caps at 128 MiB in Chrome WebGPU.

M4.rt_note: 2nd gen Apple hardware RT. Higher throughput than M3.

A17_Pro.rt_note: Same HW RT as M3, mobile variant. First iPhone with HW ray tracing.

apple_family_8.rt_note: Metal RT API available but runs on shader cores (software). No dedicated RT hardware.

apple_family_7.rt_note: No hardware RT. Software ray tracing only.

apple_known_bugs_quirks: maxStorageBufferBindingSize capped at 128 MiB in Chrome WebGPU on Metal backend
Unified memory means GPU and CPU compete for bandwidth
Metal 3 features (mesh shading, HW RT) not all exposed through WebGPU yet
M1/M2: no hardware RT -- software ray tracing only; M3+ has HW RT
iOS: WebGPU only in Safari (WebKit) or WKWebView apps. Chrome WebGPU NOT available on iOS.
iOS thermal throttling reduces sustained GPU performance by 30-40%
A16: only 6 GB unified memory shared with system -- very limited for complex scenes

macos.note: macOS Metal: BC supported. NO ETC2 or ASTC.

ios.note: iOS Metal: ETC2 + ASTC supported. NO BC. Must ship ASTC variants for iPhone.

apple_optimal_workgroup_size.note: 32 SIMD width; 64 = 2 SIMD groups. Apple recommends multiples of 32. On mobile (A-series), 32 reduces register pressure.

qualcomm_adreno.wave_note: Adreno 7xx uses 64-wide waves. Some modes support 128-wide.
qualcomm_adreno.shader_f16_blocker: Adreno devices cannot surface 16-bit values in uniform/storage buffers. This blocks the WebGPU shader-f16 feature entirely (gpuweb issue #5006). ALL Qualcomm Adreno GPUs are affected.

texture_formats.note: Mobile Android: ETC2 + ASTC. No BC.

webgpu_limits_common.maxComputeWorkGroupSizeZ_note: Adreno limits Z dimension to 64, not 1024.
webgpu_limits_common.note: Android Chrome WebGPU. Requires Chrome 121+ and Android 12+.

Adreno_740.rt_note: First Qualcomm GPU with hardware ray tracing.

Adreno_730.rt_note: No hardware ray tracing. Software RT only.

known_bugs_quirks: shader-f16 NOT available on ANY Qualcomm Adreno device in WebGPU
Buffer size limitations (128 MiB) make large model/scene deployment impossible
maxComputeWorkGroupSizeZ limited to 64 -- avoid deep Z workgroups
Snapdragon 8 Gen 1 (Adreno 730) notorious for thermal throttling and power inefficiency
Qualcomm Vulkan drivers historically buggier than desktop vendors
Some early driver versions incorrectly report Vulkan feature support
Thermal throttling reduces sustained performance by 40-60% on phones

arm_mali.wave_note: Arm Valhall and 5th Gen GPUs use 16-wide warps. FMA and SVT pipelines are 16-wide. SFU pipeline is 4-wide (sin/cos/rsqrt 4x slower).
arm_mali.shader_f16_note: Mali GPUs have native FP16 support. 2x FP16 throughput per lane. Can use 32x fp16 ops per clock per warp.

texture_formats.note: Mobile Mali: ETC2 + ASTC. ASTC hardware decoding is very efficient.

webgpu_limits_common.maxComputeInvocationsPerWorkGroup_note: Mali GPUs have 512 max invocations per workgroup, NOT 1024. Critical for path tracer workgroup sizing.

Mali_G720_Immortalis.rt_note: 2nd gen Arm hardware RT. Immortalis variant ONLY -- Mali-G720 without Immortalis has NO RT.
Mali_G720_Immortalis.tflops_fp32_note: Estimated: 12 cores x 2 EUs x 256 FP32 ops/cycle x ~940 MHz. Varies by SoC.

Mali_G715_Immortalis.rt_note: 1st gen Arm hardware RT (Immortalis variant). First Arm GPU with HW ray tracing. Mali-G715 without Immortalis has NO RT.
Mali_G715_Immortalis.tflops_fp32_note: Estimated: 11 cores x 2 EUs x 256 FP32 ops/cycle x ~850 MHz.

known_bugs_quirks: maxComputeInvocationsPerWorkGroup is 512, NOT 1024 -- size workgroups accordingly
16-wide subgroup size means subgroup operations behave differently than 32/64-wide GPUs
Arm proprietary Mali driver vs PanVK open-source driver can have different behavior
SFU pipeline only 4-wide -- sin/cos/rsqrt are 4x slower than FMA operations
Immortalis vs Mali branding: ONLY Immortalis variants have hardware ray tracing
Thermal throttling on mobile; sustained performance ~50-60% of peak
Mali-G720 is 15% faster than G715 architecturally, plus 40% less memory bandwidth usage

samsung_xclipse.wave_note: RDNA 3 based -- same Wave32/64 as desktop AMD. Likely defaults to Wave32 on mobile.
samsung_xclipse.shader_f16_note: RDNA 3 has native FP16. Samsung driver should expose it, but WebGPU availability depends on Chrome/Android implementation.

texture_formats.note: Android device: ETC2 + ASTC. Despite being RDNA 3 architecture, follows Android norms.

webgpu_limits_common.note: RDNA 3 architecture means same compute limits as desktop AMD.

vulkan_limits_common.note: Samsung's Vulkan driver for RDNA 3 -- may differ from AMD's desktop driver in practice.

Xclipse_940.rt_note: RDNA 3 Ray Accelerator, mobile variant. Hardware BVH traversal + intersections.
Xclipse_940.tflops_fp32_note: 768 shaders at ~1.1 GHz. ~70% faster than Xclipse 920.
Xclipse_940.device_note: Exynos 2400 used in Galaxy S24 in some regions (not global).

known_bugs_quirks: Samsung's custom RDNA 3 Vulkan driver is NOT the same as AMD's desktop driver
Driver quality historically worse than AMD desktop or Qualcomm mobile
10% slower than Adreno 750 in Vulkan benchmarks
FidelityFX Super Resolution support (AMD technology)
Thermal throttling significant on Galaxy S24
VK_EXT_subgroup_size_control exposure uncertain on Samsung's RDNA 3 driver
Exynos-only markets (not US) -- limited testing coverage in WebGPU implementations

mobile_optimal_workgroup_size.note: 64 is safe across all mobile GPUs. Mali max is 512 total invocations. Adreno Z-dim max is 64.

xe_hpg_alchemist.rt_note: Hardware BVH traversal and ray/box intersection per Xe-core. Competitive with RDNA 2 RT.
xe_hpg_alchemist.wave_note: Xe-HPG supports SIMD8, SIMD16, SIMD32 via VK_EXT_subgroup_size_control. Default is SIMD16.

webgpu_limits_common.maxStorageBufferBindingSize_note: Arc GPUs may report up to 1 GiB binding size.
webgpu_limits_common.note: D3D12 backend on Windows, Vulkan on Linux.

xe_lp_integrated.rt_note: Xe-LP has no hardware ray tracing. Only Xe-HPG (Arc) has RT hardware.
xe_lp_integrated.wave_note: Same Xe subgroup sizes as Xe-HPG.

vulkan_limits_common.note: Some Tiger Lake configs report 512 maxComputeWorkGroupInvocations.

webgpu_limits_common.note: Integrated GPU. D3D12 on Windows, Vulkan on Linux.

Iris_Xe_TigerLake_96EU.eu_note: 96 EUs in i7 models. 80 EUs in i5. 48 in some i5/i3.
Iris_Xe_TigerLake_96EU.memory_bandwidth_note: LPDDR4X-4267 ~68 GB/s. DDR4-3200 ~51.2 GB/s. Varies by system.

Iris_Xe_AlderLake_96EU.eu_note: 96 EUs in i7-12700H etc. 80 EUs in some i5 configs.

intel_known_bugs_quirks: 4 GB per-buffer allocation limit on Arc GPUs (compute-runtime issue #627)
shaderStorageImageMultisample not supported on Arc -- blocks some applications
Vulkan driver maturity behind AMD/NVIDIA -- occasional device lost errors
ReBAR (Resizable BAR) strongly recommended for Arc performance
D3D9/DX11 translation layer on Arc (D3D12 underneath) causes compatibility issues
Subgroup matrix operations available via experimental chromium extension (chromium_experimental_subgroup_matrix)
Intel ANV driver (Linux) vs Windows Intel driver may expose different limits
Iris Xe integrated: shared memory severely limits path tracing bandwidth
Some Tiger Lake configs report 512 maxComputeWorkGroupInvocations
Keep drivers current -- Intel Arc driver updates significantly improve stability

intel_texture_formats.note: Desktop Intel (both Arc and Iris Xe): BC only.

intel_optimal_workgroup_size.note: SIMD16 default. 64 = 4 subgroups for Arc. Iris Xe integrated: 32 to reduce register pressure.

bc_formats.note: BC (S3TC/DXT) on all desktop. NOT on mobile.

etc2_astc_formats.note: ETC2 + ASTC on all mobile. Not on desktop macOS/Windows.

texture_format_matrix.recommendation: Use Basis Universal / KTX2 supercompressed textures that transcode to BC on desktop and ASTC on mobile.

shader_f16_support_matrix.nvidia_pascal: exposed but no perf benefit (runs at FP32 rate)
shader_f16_support_matrix.note: Qualcomm Adreno is the ONLY major GPU family that CANNOT use shader-f16 in WebGPU. The blocker is lack of 16-bit value support in uniform/storage buffers.

subgroup_size_summary.note: Write shaders that work with any subgroup size for portability. Subgroup operations in WebGPU still experimental.

browser_support_status.safari: Stable since Safari 18 (Sep 2024). macOS and iOS.
browser_support_status.firefox: Nightly only as of early 2026. ~90% spec compliance. 3 full-time devs.

common_pitfalls_for_path_tracers: maxStorageBufferBindingSize is 128 MiB default -- BVH/scene data may exceed this. Request higher limit.
maxStorageBuffersPerShaderStage is 8 default -- path tracers often need more. Request up to 10 (Chrome 120+) or 16 (Chrome 146+).
Mali GPUs have 512 maxComputeInvocationsPerWorkGroup, not 1024. Size workgroups accordingly.
Adreno maxComputeWorkGroupSizeZ is 64, not 1024. Avoid deep Z workgroups on mobile.
No shader-f16 on Qualcomm -- cannot use f16 for perf optimization on most Android flagships.
iOS has NO BC textures. Must ship ASTC variants for iPhone.
Texture sampling in compute shaders: performance varies wildly across vendors.
Chrome on laptops may use integrated GPU instead of discrete. powerPreference hint does not always work.
Thermal throttling on mobile reduces sustained path tracing performance by 30-60%.
Buffer size limits on mobile (128 MiB) can prevent loading complex scenes.
WebGPU does NOT expose hardware ray tracing -- all path tracing must be in compute shaders regardless of GPU.
AMD Vulkan default subgroup size is 64 (not native 32) -- test both via VK_EXT_subgroup_size_control.
Mali SFU pipeline is 4-wide -- sin/cos/rsqrt are 4x slower than FMA. Minimize transcendentals on Mali.
